expected_discriminator.inconclusive: The new oracle still overflows before covering the target window, codeword slide is not acceptable, or rollback/selftest fails.
expected_discriminator.negative: The expanded/filtered capture covers the post-FWREADY execute_start window without overflow hiding target candidates and still reports zero target hits.
expected_discriminator.positive: A higher-coverage or filtered execute_start capture finds at least one firmware_class/qcacld-HDD target function or stack/callsite hit.

next_runner_contract: Do not rerun workspace/private/inputs/boot_images/boot_linux_v2277_workqueue_exec_stack.img for the same question.

Build a new V2279 source artifact that changes coverage: larger sample map/print limit, bounded full-print transfer, or source-backed in-BPF/userland filtering for target-relevant execute_start records.
Keep the same V2276 same-boot codeword acceptance policy and collect a paired codeword log.
If live validation is needed after the build, use only native_init_flash.py, rollback to V2237 or the selected baseline, and verify selftest fail=0.
Do not run Wi-Fi scan/connect/DHCP/ping; the embedded boot_wlan path is sufficient for this oracle.
Publish only redacted counts, target-hit summaries, and coverage metrics; keep raw stack/IP logs private.
candidates.summary: V2278 proved the V2277 live route and rollback, but the target-negative result only covers the printed workqueue window because 5213 execute_start events overflowed. The next unit should build a higher-coverage or filtered execute_start oracle, not rerun V2277.
why_independent: It does not repeat the V2277 image or the V2278 printed-window capture.
It addresses the actual V2278 limitation: 512 printed samples versus 6237 total events and 5213 overflowed events.
It remains on the same T1 question but changes the observable coverage boundary, so a target hit cannot be hidden behind print/storage overflow.
